domain: Sherpa Khumbu Icefall route (Everest base-camp; Icefall Doctors install cascading fixed-rope + lashed-aluminum-ladder bridges across 100-150m crevasses; mandatory carabiner clip-in to new anchor BEFORE unclip from previous — never unanchored)
specific_mechanism: Cascading multi-hop reasoning with explicit per-step anchor_check probe + MANDATORY OVERLAP (step k+1's input includes step k's anchor_check output) + dynamic K-step cascade length determined by step controller.
llm_application: SHERPA-RAPPEL: a cascading multi-hop reasoning protocol. Each reasoning step k produces (a) output_k, (b) anchor_check_k = self-consistency probe verifying step k's claim against original input and previous outputs, (c) next-step-input formed by combining output_k AND anchor_check_k. Step k+1 cannot proceed unless anchor_check_k passes a calibrated confidence threshold (CLIP-IN to new anchor before UNCLIPPING from previous). Final answer = output_K where K is dynamically chosen by step controller monitoring marginal-information-gain. Differs from generic CoT (no per-step anchor probe), Chain-of-X (no mandatory anchor-overlap input), CoT-RAG (RAG-only checkpoint), Sketch-of-Thought (sketch not anchor probe) by combining (a) per-step explicit anchor_check, (b) MANDATORY OVERLAP, (c) dynamic K-step controller.
novelty_claim: Chain-of-X survey + CoT-faithfulness measurement + CoT-RAG + Sketch-of-Thought already cover per-step verification + adaptive reasoning. SHERPA-RAPPEL recombines per-step anchor + mandatory overlap. Recombination of existing components.
